title: 'Alien Romulus' Global Box Office Screams to Massive Milestone
text: Disney/20th Century's latest sci-fi horror installment has achieved a significant commercial milestone, crossing the $300 million threshold at the global box office. The film garnered approximately $13.7 million internationally over its weekend, bringing its total to $217.2 million from international markets and $314.4 million worldwide. Directed by Fede Álvarez, the project stars Cailee Spaeny, David Jonsson, Archie Renaux, Isabela Merced, and Spike Fearn. The narrative follows a group of young space colonists who encounter terrifying extraterrestrial creatures while exploring a derelict spacecraft in their bid to escape for a better existence. As is typical for the franchise, circumstances deteriorate rapidly. The film has maintained strong critical reception, currently holding an 80% approval rating on Rotten Tomatoes. Set two decades after the original installment, it currently ranks as the second-highest-grossing entry in the series. While the project remains nearly $100 million behind the franchise leader, it has substantially outperformed 2017's "Alien: Covenant." The original 1979 film earned $186 million, equivalent to approximately $291 million in contemporary dollars when adjusted for inflation. Its continued theatrical success suggests potential continuation of the franchise.
